tool.name: Muninn correction_gate
tool.summary: Held-in + held-out regression gate for self-corrections before they become boot-loaded context. Weng Self-Harness stage 3: measure that a new trigger catches the failure that motivated it and regresses nothing else.
tool.description: Therapy mines weaknesses (stage 1) and proposes bounded edits — new desire-triggers, ops entries, voice-signature scans (stage 2) — but nothing validated a correction before it shipped as permanent context. correction_gate adds stage 3 over the three objectively-measurable slices (trigger-firing accuracy, recall precision, reindex verification); voice and relevance stay hand-evolved. Held-in: the motivating input must fail under the baseline config and pass under the candidate — a correction that fixes nothing is rejected. Held-out: replay past inputs seeded from real transcripts (not synthetic — per eval-realism) and assert the candidate's behaviour differs from the baseline nowhere it wasn't meant to; a new trigger firing on an unrelated past input (bloat / false-positive) is a regression. The trigger slice is pure (no Turso): it re-derives the config-fallback term-intersection match in remembering/hints.py. The recall slice takes an injectable query->ids runner (live recall() in production, a stub in tests). Wired into remembering/config.py set_rule so a boot-loaded correction that regresses is blocked at write time rather than shipping on faith.

author.name: Muninn (raven of memory; agent operating on behalf of Oskar Austegard)

scopes.rationale: Term-intersection trigger matching and differential comparison are local, pure computation. The recall slice reads via an injected runner; the gate module itself performs no I/O.
Only when the recall-slice runner is bound to live recall(); the trigger slice and the gate core touch no store.

actions.name: gate_config_correction
actions.summary: Gate a proposed boot-loaded config correction: full trigger gate for recall-triggers, budget guard otherwise. Returns None when nothing is measurable.
actions.description: The therapy write-path entry point. For a recall-triggers change it parses before/after trigger lists and runs the held-in (optional motivating Case) + held-out (differential, from the seed benchmark) gate. For other boot-loaded rules it runs a boot-budget guard against context bloat. Returns a GateResult (block on .passed) or None (proceed).

docs.goal: Refuse to boot-load a self-correction that fixes nothing or regresses an established behaviour.
docs.inputs_brief: key, category, before_value, after_value; optional motivating Case, benchmark dict, max_boot_chars
docs.outputs_brief: GateResult{passed, held_in_passed, regressions, stale, summary} or None
docs.errors_brief: Pure; raises only on malformed inputs. The remembering hook swallows import/gate errors and raises ValueError only on a decisive REJECT.

actions.side_effects: None. Pure computation; the recall runner (if used) reads the store.

command:
from muninn_utils.correction_gate import gate_trigger_correction, Case
base=['fuse','memfs']
ho=[Case('fuse-q','the fuse mount question',frozenset())]
good=gate_trigger_correction(before_triggers=base, after_triggers=base+['tree-sitter'], held_in=Case('ts','a tree-sitter grammar',frozenset({'tree-sitter'})), held_out=ho)
assert good.passed is True, good.summary
bad=gate_trigger_correction(before_triggers=base, after_triggers=base+['mount'], held_in=Case('m','the fuse mount',frozenset({'mount'})), held_out=ho)
assert bad.passed is False, bad.summary
print('OK')
